tweets.text: RT @socialistlib510: Jeremy Hammond, you're a fucking hero.
Got off the phone with my boss. I need to get a bunch of stuff done before I leave for my trip next week.
@poezn Nice and tall, just how I like 'em.
Animating text paths with this #delta #tributary here - this stuff is addictive.. http://t.co/h4PB2VtY
RT @FreeJeremyNet: Jeremy's twin brother, Jason, says 15 mos. is enough time in jail. Bring him home. Sign the petition. #FreeJeremy https:…
RT @FreeJeremyNet: Jason Hammond and the legal team talk to the press outside court. #FreeJeremy #FreeHammond http://t.co/tmHNi1VJUN
RT @ptvan: @hlftn answers the question "Why is the sky blue?" with this animation http://t.co/7baEpsv6BG

@visakanv @vvthreads @round This is related to the reason I added a linkable backup of all of my book highlights to https://t.co/2h5nARGM1I, because now I can thread passages of books easily into this web of inter-related ideas, like this one: https://t.co/GonLjjQIEk https://t.co/xEbr7KFs3u

I'll add more of these as I create them. This is a sort of backup, meta-representation of these trains of thought I have so I can thread Twitter ideas in. 

Inspired by smart threading by folks like @visakanv / @vvthreads and @round  https://t.co/NUYQjSx2Ix

🧠 Neo-Internet Philosophy represents a new point of view I've been exploring lately, returning to the roots of the world wide web, the command line, cryptography, and open-source approaches as a response to the state of the web in 2019 https://t.co/YC8DKolvmm

🔨 The Twitter Justice System is a small but growing collection of stories about how Twitter distributes justice and what that means about free speech and discourse on the internet. https://t.co/AbwJu0P9rE
🔨 Technological Mutual Aid is for research I have been doing lately about using the internet for Good and not Evil, ways that technology can make the world better.  https://t.co/ftLRqwcxVv

📓 Creations is where I collect my original work, sketches and photography and art I've made. I'm trying to get out of the habit of using Instagram as my only record of work. I'm not particularly proud of any of these, don't look. https://t.co/bKgGwOfBGp / https://t.co/i0F4UcwCIo

Inspired in large parts by George Hotz's talk at SXSW about jailbreaking the simulation, I've been collecting related ideas in 💻 Disassociative Matrix Theory - this is the channel I assume Neo would be working on when we first meet him in the Matrix https://t.co/xM1t4EUU6R

I've been collecting things that feel 
💻 c̶̪̥͈̋̇̈́͒́̑̕͝y̶̡̧͔̹͉̯̮͚͎̽̅̔̈́̿̀͠ḅ̷̧̧͈̝̺̰̥̽͘e̷̡̙͙͙̝͋̈́̄̒r̶͇͆̾͜p̶̡͕͉̺̘̤̱̳̙͙̂͗́ù̴̖̿́̆̊̿̊̈́ņ̸̬̼͉̟͒k̵̢̺̠͎̝̳̙̟̿̅̾͊͌̌͜ in https://t.co/Ci9BuPFM5H - images, screenshots, quotes, news articles, music.
🎨 type is my cliche designer typeface inspiration moodboard https://t.co/zfIA0gUeZV

🎨 Urban Sketching is a representation of my current obsession with urban drawing and sketching, and I have been updating this more than any other channel, lately – almost always directly from artists I follow on instagram https://t.co/cbuBjH2IeH

🎧 Favorite podcast episodes will be where I put the best-of-the-best podcast episodes I find

https://t.co/MJfhPX77OX
🍁2020 Weed Consumption is a collection of loose ideas around how legalization is changing how we approach cannabis and it's consumption as a society https://t.co/vkY4JN7rH7

My 🌞 vibes 🖤 channel https://t.co/iWXt277F5G is a mood board and visual inspiration dumping ground, which is how I used to use Tumblr. It also powers the Vibes part of my website: https://t.co/6HEQNN1YL9

I've been collecting meta-thoughts on https://t.co/77c24W8zC4 in this channel: https://t.co/KExqQHZFdf - including categorization/emoji key, and thoughts on threading and ideas around linking ideas and pieces of the internet together

https://t.co/UAJChFbyhF, like all great websites, is whatever you make it. I've been discovering and inspired by really smart designers and thinkers, and I love how it lets you see other people's thought processes in public. https://t.co/itH84ZWfwU

I've been really enjoying using https://t.co/UAJChFbyhF as a healthy lens through which to experience and think about the internet. It takes consciously switching from "scrolling feeds" to "doing research" and it reminds me of why I love the internet. https://t.co/x7Q9SVDz4m

nothing matters, send italian care package, havent had pizza in months https://t.co/ypusBvt9cM
i just left, pretending i don't even have a kitchen anymore

4:00, starving, haven't eaten all day, go into my fridge and reach for the last thing in there, some okay-looking leftovers from last night, not great but it's what i got, place it on the counter, but not really, it falls dramatically, every single piece of food hits the floor
My (thus unmusical) friend's raps on his MySpace are actually really good and entertaining. And awesome. Straight up awesome.
Anyway the point is I need to write some code.

They were like, okay, here is this internet thing, its pretty powerful, and we are pretty sure as long as we dont centralize the power to control it in the hands of a few people, we should be fine, and we have engineered the infrastructure to reflect that. Cut to 40 years later.

Gotta write some code so I can move onto writing ten million words to myself about the death of Twitter and about our inability to treat our online shared spaces with any respect for the history of social organization/governance, even though the cypherpunks predicted all of this
Trafficking in my wares. My design wares.
Launched Photoshop to work on a cover for Hack This Zine and "Anarchy In The UK" comes on. Which is a good sign.
Doing my first ever magazine layout in InDesign. Kinda... uh... wow.
Oh hey look at that. SVN is actually pretty cool.
